businessModel.text: Revenue from Frito-Lay (snacks North America, ~25%), PepsiCo Beverages North America (~25%), Latin America (~20%), Europe (~15%), and Africa/Asia, Australia, China and New Zealand (~15%).

recentCatalysts.text: Dividend aristocrat: 53 consecutive years of dividend increases; yield ~2.7% $7bn annual buyback programme provides ongoing capital return floor Latin America and Africa emerging markets growing high-single-digit organic revenue Amazon distribution partnership expanding for direct-to-consumer snack delivery
items: Dividend aristocrat: 53 consecutive years of dividend increases; yield ~2.7%
$7bn annual buyback programme provides ongoing capital return floor
Latin America and Africa emerging markets growing high-single-digit organic revenue
Amazon distribution partnership expanding for direct-to-consumer snack delivery

keyRisks.text: 1. US beverage and snack volumes negative FY25: consumer trading down in key categories 2. Mexico (Femsa) refranchising overhang: pending transaction could create supply-chain disruption 3. Commodity input costs (corn, sugar, aluminium) elevated — 2-year hedged but FY27 exposure 4. Hart-Scott-Rodino antitrust review on streaming/subscription beverage bundles 5. EU sugary-drink tax expansion reducing revenue-per-litre in European markets

risks.risk: 1. US beverage and snack volumes negative FY25: consumer trading down in key categories
2. Mexico (Femsa) refranchising overhang: pending transaction could create supply-chain disruption
3. Commodity input costs (corn, sugar, aluminium) elevated — 2-year hedged but FY27 exposure
4. Hart-Scott-Rodino antitrust review on streaming/subscription beverage bundles
5. EU sugary-drink tax expansion reducing revenue-per-litre in European markets

whoShouldOwn.text: Defensive income portfolio anchor for investors requiring yield and dividend longevity. NOT suitable for investors seeking growth or those expecting commodity tailwinds for the snack and beverage portfolio.

entryExit.text: BUY/HOLD: Fair value zone $148–$158 at current growth rates. | REDUCE above $168.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via DYOR HQ data pipeline (Google Sheets + Yahoo Finance). Fields include price, market capitalisation, 52-week range, P/E, EPS, and volume.
sources.companyFilings: Public company filings, regulatory announcements, investor presentations, and RNS where referenced in the analysis.
